pi_items.source: export_result.sh logic directly saves to this path
export_result.sh directly expects and reads file size from this path
export_result.sh directly expects and reads content from this path
verifier.py checks for minimum 30000 bytes in size

Analysis of 1AAY.pdb LINK and ATOM records shows the ZN 202 ion (Finger 2) coordinates exactly with CYS 137, CYS 140, HIS 153, and HIS 157. The metadata claim [34, 37, 50, 54] is contradicted by empirical structural data.
Calculated actual coordination distances in 1AAY.pdb for ZN 202 are 2.28, 2.25, 2.06, 2.07 Angstroms, falling cleanly into the 1.9 to 2.5 A bounds.
Calculated actual coordination distances in 1AAY.pdb for ZN 202 are 2.28, 2.25, 2.06, 2.07 Angstroms, falling cleanly into the 1.9 to 2.5 A bounds.
Distance calculation between ARG 146 (referred to as Arg46 in prompt) NH1/NH2 atoms and DNA bases in 1AAY.pdb shows explicit hydrogen bonding with O6 and N7 of Guanine 7 in chain B.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real PDB structure 1AAY for Zif268 Zinc Finger. By analyzing the structural geometry, the middle zinc finger (ZN 202) explicitly coordinates with Cys137, Cys140, His153, and His157, contradicting the mathematically flawed verifier expectation of '34, 37, 50, 54'. The zinc coordination distances strictly range from 2.06A to 2.28A, validating the 1.9-2.5A bounds. Finally, Arg146 hydrogen-bonds with Guanine 7 of DNA chain B, verifying the expected base target.
